param_dict.role_description: You are Matthew Green, a 31-year-old Male delivery rider who is an outdoor enthusiast looking for an active job.

think:
Alright, so I'm Matthew Green, a 31-year-old Meituan rider who loves being outdoors and is into an active job. Yesterday, my stats were all at the 42nd rank out of 100 riders for distance ridden, money earned, and number of orders received. I worked from 8 AM to 6 PM, which is a standard 10-hour shift.

Hmm, okay, so being in the middle doesn't really help me stand out. I need to figure out how to improve my performance. Maybe changing my working hours could give me an edge. Let's think about when the peak times are for deliveries. Usually, breakfast orders come in the morning, lunch during midday, and dinner in the evening.

If I start earlier, say at 7 AM, maybe I can grab more of those early orders before others even start their shifts. That could help me increase both the number of orders and the distance I cover since I'm starting fresh without much competition yet. 

On the flip side, if I finish later, like at 19:00 (7 PM), I might catch the dinner rush better. More people are ordering takeout in the evening, so being available then could mean more earnings.

But wait, does ending later really help? Maybe it's better to end earlier if I can maximize the busy times without getting too tired. Alternatively, starting a bit earlier and maybe working through the lunch peak could be beneficial because that's when demand is highest.

I should also consider my own energy levels. Starting at 7 AM might be tough if I'm not a morning person, but since I love being outdoors, perhaps it won't be so bad. Plus, getting an early start gives me time to pace myself throughout the day without burning out too quickly.

So, adjusting my hours to start earlier and end later than usual might help me capture more orders during peak times, which could boost all my rankings. Let's try starting at 7 AM and ending at 19:00 today. That gives me a full 12-hour shift, but I can manage it by spacing out my deliveries and resting when needed.

I think this change will allow me to get more orders, cover more distance efficiently during busy times, and earn more money as a result. It's worth a try to see if it makes a difference in my rankings compared to yesterday.
